response.etymologyXML: [Middle English doun, from Old English -dūne (as in ofdūne, downwards), from dūne, dative of dūn, hill; see dheuə- in Indo-European roots.]
[Middle English doune, from Old English dūn, hill; see dheuə- in Indo-European roots.]
